surface: Private Milestone 2 source implementation: data-only Lynx renderer configuration, DOM-free universal compiler ABI, a background root/client driver, named-ContextProxy async transport, a root-scoped main-thread Element PAPI receiver, acknowledgement-gated cloned ref handles, async teardown, and renderer-local declarations for the first ten planned native tags. The official JavaScript environment covers mount, update, keyed reorder, fault, and unmount; no production .lynx.bundle or native-device claim is made.
divergences: The package adapts an audited @lynx-js/types@4.0.0 props/events slice into renderer-owned module declarations because the upstream events entry transitively loads global Element/JSX augmentation.
Background refs receive cloned acknowledgement-gated identity handles, never live main-thread Element PAPI objects; query and UI methods remain deferred to Milestone 3.
Main-thread receiver installation is explicit in this private source phase; automatic Rspeedy production bootstrap is deferred to Milestone 5.
Platform and public testing-helper runtime APIs remain absent instead of being represented by throwing or inert compatibility stubs.
ssr: Unsupported. Lynx first-screen adoption is a native runtime protocol, not HTML SSR or Octane hydration.
notes: Milestone 0 remains blocked on public lifecycle/event hooks, reload/background teardown, Web execution, and the Android/iOS engine matrix; this private implementation is not a technical preview.

Native event binding, complete props/styles/query behavior, and core elements remain Milestone 3; platform capabilities remain Milestone 4; production testing/.lynx.bundle integration remains Milestone 5.
